title: Apply to import a vehicle
description: Get permission to bring a car, truck or motorcycle into Barbados.
summary: You must get permission from the Barbados Licensing Authority before you bring a vehicle into Barbados. The Licensing Authority needs to know about you, the vehicle, where it is coming from and how it will be used.
An import permit is separate from the customs duty you pay when the vehicle actually arrives. The Barbados Revenue Authority handles the duty. The Licensing Authority handles the permit.

If you qualify for a duty-free concession – for example, as a returning national or a person with a disability – you can apply for that as well so you do not pay customs duty. See ‘Apply for a duty-free vehicle concession’.
The form is called the Application for the Importation of Vehicles into Barbados. You need it whether the vehicle is new or used, and whether it is coming by sea or by air.

items: your full name, address and contact details
the country the vehicle is coming from
the make, model and year of the vehicle
the chassis number, engine number and cubic capacity
the body type, colour and seating capacity
how you will use the vehicle (private, public service, goods, etc.)
the name of the shipping company and the expected date of arrival

items.name: Export certificate from the country of origin

fees: An import permit application fee is payable to the Barbados Licensing Authority. You will also need to pay customs duties to the Barbados Revenue Authority when the vehicle arrives.
how_long: An import permit decision takes around 2 to 4 weeks.

options.heading: In person
content: Take the Application for the Importation of Vehicles into Barbados form, plus your supporting documents, to the Barbados Licensing Authority.

contact.address:
The Pine, East–West Boulevard
St. Michael
BB11000

assumptions: Processing time of ‘2 to 4 weeks’ is an estimate. The form does not state how long a decision takes.
‘An import permit application fee is payable’ is inferred – the form does not state a fee.
Document list (bill of sale, export certificate, bill of lading, photo ID) is the common BLA practice. The form itself does not enumerate the documents you must attach.
